subject: Modify dependencies to avoid enabled features fail
commit_message:
Modify dependencies to avoid enabled features fail

The drl change is only partial just to recognize Tosca
policies.

Change-Id: I5d3b8071394a9ea14638170e04d74e4c6b4ad028
Issue-ID: POLICY-1642
Signed-off-by: jhh <jorge.hernandez-herrero@att.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Verified+1

Patch Set 1:

After all latest changes in apps/models repo - the "dublin" controller does not come up.  Needed to play it with dependencies to make it come up.

Patch Set 1:

Please let's merge this one if you don't mind, I need to test with the image.

Patch Set 1:

If you don't have that in, then it will not override the oparent value which is false.

Patch Set 1:

I think this is a global issue Pam, as this code is plain copy/paste across repos.   See here, all these repos below have the wrong value, so they don't override the value that you mentioned from parent to enable it, so in theory they are disabled.   The issue is present on models, common, drools-applications, and drools-pdp from the repos I cloned.

Tricky thing, is that at this stage, 1 day before M4, we are going to have it inadvertently disabled to have it enabled, and that may lead to a bunch of issues to be dealt with, which is not better usage of time today, because of M4 tomorrow.

I think we should open a ticket to track it, and have new comers into the platform willing to help to do the work across the board post M4.   What do you think?  If we go for the ticket not sure how should be handled perhaps as a task provided m4 is tomorrow?

Ok – there needs to be major cleanup in this repo for it. None of these POM’s should have this, it should only be in the root pom.xml
 
So let’s defer to next release.
